intro: Mesembryanthemum vanrensburgii, commonly known as the Namaqua water biscuit, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throat, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Mesembryanthemum vanrensburgii include flavonoids, saponins, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
